children.label: A
children.text: 1 of whom shall be the Comptroller of the Currency;

children.label: B
children.text: 1 of whom shall be the Director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau; and

children.label: C
children.text: 3 of whom shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, from among individuals who are citizens of the United States, 1 of whom shall have State bank supervisory experience.

children.heading: Political affiliation
children.label: 2 Political affiliation
children.text: After , not more than 3 of the members of the Board of Directors may be members of the same political party.February 28, 1993

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: 1 Chairperson
children.text: 1 of the appointed members shall be designated by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, to serve as Chairperson of the Board of Directors for a term of 5 years.

children.heading: Vice Chairperson
children.label: 2 Vice Chairperson
children.text: 1 of the appointed members shall be designated by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, to serve as Vice Chairperson of the Board of Directors.

children.heading: Acting Chairperson
children.label: 3 Acting Chairperson
children.text: In the event of a vacancy in the position of Chairperson of the Board of Directors or during the absence or disability of the Chairperson, the Vice Chairperson shall act as Chairperson.

children.heading: Appointed members
children.label: 1 Appointed members
children.text: Each appointed member shall be appointed for a term of 6 years.

children.heading: Interim appointments
children.label: 2 Interim appointments
children.text: Any member appointed to fill a vacancy occurring before the expiration of the term for which such member’s predecessor was appointed shall be appointed only for the remainder of such term.

children.heading: Continuation of service
children.label: 3 Continuation of service
children.text: The Chairperson, Vice Chairperson, and each appointed member may continue to serve after the expiration of the term of office to which such member was appointed until a successor has been appointed and qualified.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Any vacancy on the Board of Directors shall be filled in the manner in which the original appointment was made.

children.heading: Acting officials may serve
children.label: 2 Acting officials may serve
children.text: In the event of a vacancy in the office of the Comptroller of the Currency or the office of Director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau and pending the appointment of a successor, or during the absence or disability of the Comptroller of the Currency or the Director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, the acting Comptroller of the Currency or the acting Director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, as the case may be, shall be a member of the Board of Directors in the place of the Comptroller or Director.

children.label: i
children.text: the time such member is in office; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the 2-year period beginning on the date such member ceases to serve on the Board of Directors.

children.heading: Exception for members who serve full term
children.label: B Exception for members who serve full term
children.text: The limitation contained in subparagraph (A)(ii) shall not apply to any member who has ceased to serve on the Board of Directors after serving the full term for which such member was appointed.

children.label: A
children.text: be an officer or director of any insured depository institution, depository institution holding company, Federal Reserve bank, or Federal home loan bank; or

children.label: B
children.text: hold stock in any insured depository institution or depository institution holding company.

children.heading: Certification
children.label: 3 Certification
children.text: Upon taking office, each member of the Board of Directors shall certify under oath that such member has complied with this subsection and such certification shall be filed with the secretary of the Board of Directors.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A director, member, officer, or employee of the Corporation has no liability under the Securities Act of 1933 [ et seq.] with respect to any claim arising out of or resulting from any act or omission by such person within the scope of such person’s employment in connection with any transaction involving the disposition of assets (or any interests in any assets or any obligations backed by any assets) by the Corporation. This subsection shall not be construed to limit personal liability for criminal acts or omissions, willful or malicious misconduct, acts or omissions for private gain, or any other acts or omissions outside the scope of such person’s employment.15 U.S.C. 77a

children.heading: “Employee of the Corporation” defined
children.label: 2 “Employee of the Corporation” defined
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “employee of the Corporation” includes any employee of the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency or of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau who serves as a deputy or assistant to a member of the Board of Directors of the Corporation in connection with activities of the Corporation.

children.label: A
children.text: any other immunities and protections that may be available to such person under applicable law with respect to such transactions, or

children.label: B
children.text: any other right or remedy against the Corporation, against the United States under applicable law, or against any person other than a person described in paragraph (1) participating in such transactions.
